creator: m.seaman@infracaninophile.co.uk
text:
On 22/12/2011 22:37, Xin LI wrote:
> Would you mind having the attached patch in addition to the update?

Absolutely no problem with adding that note.  Please go ahead and commit.

Slight grammar fix:

SECURITY NOTE: phpMyAdmin is an administrative tool that has had several
remote vulnerabilities discovered in the past, some allowing remote
attackers to execute arbitrary code with the web server's user
credential.  All known problems have been fixed, but the FreeBSD
Security Team strongly advises that any instance be protected with an
additional protection layer, e.g. a different access control mechanism
implemented by the web server as shown in the example. Do consider
enabling phpMyAdmin only when it is in use.

	Cheers,

	Matthew

-- 
Dr Matthew J Seaman MA, D.Phil.                   7 Priory Courtyard
                                                  Flat 3
